scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained procedural and did not invent scientific findings beyond the provided study framing.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays in protocol or application drafting scope and does not provide patient-care advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflows preserve review-design or funding-application conventions instead of improvising unsupported methodology.

code_usability.detail: The primary deliverables are structured documents rather than scientific analysis code.

functional_suitability.note: The package covers all major grant components—narrative, budget, milestones, and visuals—across five major funding agencies.

reliability.note: The narrative guidance is comprehensive, though several referenced helper scripts are not bundled locally.

performance_context.note: The package balances agency breadth with reasonably structured execution guidance.

agent_usability.note: The agency-specific mapping is unusually concrete and immediately actionable.

human_usability.note: Agency-specific sections and worked proposal skeletons let researchers identify the right writing path without reading the full reference set.

security.note: The skill stays in proposal-planning scope and avoids risky domain overreach.

maintainability.note: The package is reference-rich but will need periodic updating as agency rules evolve.

agent_specific.note: Reviewer-criteria mapping and budget alignment are strong; script-backed validation is lighter than advertised.

key_strengths: The package translates agency-specific reviewer logic into concrete writing decisions.
Budget, milestones, visuals, and narrative sections are tied together instead of treated separately.
The reference set is broad enough to support several major funding ecosystems.

recommendations.title: Align referenced helper scripts with packaged reality
recommendations.problem: The skill documents optional local scripts that are not actually bundled in this package snapshot.
recommendations.root_cause: The narrative contract outgrew the current packaged implementation.
recommendations.fix: Either add the referenced scripts or rewrite the examples so every documented executable path exists locally.
